events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Richard Starkey, known as Ringo Starr, was born on July 7, 1940, in Liverpool, England, and rose to international fame as the drummer for The Beatles.

angles.name: Drummer legend
angles.copy: The man who kept the beat for the biggest band in the world is 86 today! Ringo Starr's drumming defined an era.

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist, songwriter, and astrophysicist Sir Brian Harold May was born on July 19, 1947.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th to the inimitable Brian May! His iconic guitar riffs powered Queen to stratospheric heights.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, the iconic frontman of The Rolling Stones, was born on July 26, 1943, in Dartford, Kent, England.

angles.name: Rock'n'Roll Icon
angles.copy: Still strutting and rocking at 83! Wishing a very happy birthday to the legendary Mick Jagger.

events.title: MTV launched, first video 'Video Killed The Radio Star'
events.blurb: On August 1, 1981, MTV: Music Television launched with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its very first music video.

angles.name: Radio's Revenge?
angles.copy: It's been 45 years since MTV launched with 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. The irony isn't lost on us.

events.title: Oasis released 'Definitely Maybe'
events.blurb: Oasis released their debut studio album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which quickly became a Britpop landmark.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: Thirty-two years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe'. Still sounds like a blast of pure Britpop swagger.

events.title: Woodstock Festival anniversary
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music and Art Fair took place from August 15-18, 1969, on a dairy farm in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million gathered for '3 Days of Peace & Music' at Woodstock. A truly iconic moment in history.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Forty-nine years since we lost The King. Elvis Presley's legacy continues to inspire generations.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's death
events.blurb: Tupac Shakur, one of hip-hop's most influential artists, was tragically shot dead in Las Vegas on September 7, 1996.

angles.name: Remembering Tupac
angles.copy: Thirty years since the music world lost Tupac Shakur. His words and impact still resonate today.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: Jimi Hendrix, widely regarded as one of the greatest guitarists of all time, passed away on September 18, 1970.

angles.name: Guitar Legend
angles.copy: Fifty-six years since we said goodbye to Jimi Hendrix. His groundbreaking guitar work changed music forever.

events.title: The Beatles released 'Abbey Road'
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic 'Abbey Road' album was released in the UK on September 26, 1969, featuring that legendary zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Iconic Album
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years since The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' hit the shelves. That album cover is still instantly recognisable.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, the formidable frontwoman of The Pretenders, was born on September 7, 1951.

angles.name: Rock Matriarch
angles.copy: Wishing a very happy 75th birthday to the one and only Chrissie Hynde! Still rocking as hard as ever.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: William John Paul Gallagher, known as Liam Gallagher, the distinctive voice of Oasis and a successful solo artist, was born on September 21, 1972, in Manchester, England.

angles.name: Still Mad For It
angles.copy: Our kid turns 54 today! Happy Birthday to the legendary Liam Gallagher – still bringing the rock 'n' roll.

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' UK single release anniversary
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic peace anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the UK in October 1975.

angles.name: Peace Anthem
angles.copy: Fifty-one years since John Lennon's 'Imagine' became a UK single. Its message of peace is as powerful today as ever.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music.

angles.name: Music in your pocket
angles.copy: Remember life before the iPod? Twenty-five years ago, Apple changed how we listened to music forever.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated annually as James Bond Day, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first Bond film, Dr. No, in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, not stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Who's your favourite 007 and what's the best Bond theme tune?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Lennon, the legendary co-founder of The Beatles and an influential solo artist, was born on October 9, 1940.

angles.name: Remembering a legend
angles.copy: Today, the iconic John Lennon would have been 86. His timeless music and message continue to inspire.
